popularStory.headline: BIO
popularStory.description:
BIO:  Inherited the Countship of Portugal.  Reported as the illegitimate daughter of Jimena Munoz in The Making of Spain (Gabriel Jackson) pp 76, 203

** from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/CASTILE.h …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal於1080年出生在葡萄牙布拉加波沃亞 德 藍索時，她的父親Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon40歲，母親Jimena Muñoz20歲。她於1094年與Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha在葡萄牙結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她於1128年登記服兵役。她去世於1130年11月11日在西班牙加利西亞武倫瑟蒙特德拉莫享年50歲，埋葬在葡萄牙布拉加布拉加布拉加 瑟。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal于1080年出生在葡萄牙布拉加波沃亞 德 藍索时，她的父亲Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon40岁，母亲Jimena Muñoz20岁。她于1094年与Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha在葡萄牙结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她于1128年登记服兵役。她去世于1130年11月11日在西班牙加利西亞武倫瑟蒙特德拉莫享年50岁，埋葬在葡萄牙布拉加布拉加布拉加 瑟。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Leon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Teresa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Theresa .  In the English-speaking world the name is often chosen in this spelling by Roman Catholics, with particular reference to the Spanish saint, Teresa of Ávila (  Teresa Cepeda de Ahumada ,  1515–82 ).

variants.name: Theresa
meanings: Of problematic origin. The name seems to have been first used in Spain and Portugal, and, according to tradition, was the name of the wife of St Paulinus of Nola, who spent most of his life in Spain; she was said to have originated (and to have derived her name) from the Greek island of Thēra. However, this story is neither factually nor etymologically confirmed.

variants.name: Teresia
meanings: Of problematic origin. The name seems to have been first used in Spain and Portugal, and, according to tradition, was the name of the wife of St Paulinus of Nola, who spent most of his life in Spain; she was said to have originated (and to have derived her name) from the Greek island of Thēra. However, this story is neither factually nor etymologically confirmed.

historicEvents.description: Afonso inherits the County of Portugal, a province of Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal was born in 1080, in Póvoa de Lanhoso, Braga, Portugal, her father, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, was 40 and her mother, Jimena Muñoz, was 20. She married Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha in 1094, in Portugal. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She registered for military service in 1128. She died on 11 November 1130, in Montederramo, Ourense, Galicia, Spain, at the age of 50, and was buried in Braga Sé, Braga, Braga, Portugal.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal est née en 1080, à Póvoa de Lanhoso, Braga, Portugal, son père, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Jimena Muñoz, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha en 1094, à Portugal. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles. Elle s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1128. Elle est décédée le 11 novembre 1130, à Montederramo, Ourense, Galicia, Espagne, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterrée à Braga Sé, Braga, Braga, Portugal.

de.lifeSummary: Als Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal 1080 in Póvoa de Lanhoso, Braga, Portugal geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon 40 und ihre Mutter Jimena Muñoz 20. Sie hat 1094 in Portugal Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie hat sich 1128 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Sie starb am 11. November 1130 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Montederramo, Ourense, Galicia, Spanien und wurde in Braga Sé, Braga, Braga, Portugal begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal è nata il nel 1080 nel Póvoa de Lanhoso, Braga, Portogallo, sua padre, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Jimena Muñoz, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha nel 1094, nel Portogallo. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 5 figlie. Lei arruolato nell'esercito nel 1128. Morì il 11 novembre 1130, nel Montederramo, Ourense, Galicia, Spagna, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolta a Braga Sé, Braga, Braga, Portogallo.

ja.lifeSummary: Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugalが1080年にポルトガルブラガプoボア ド ランhオソで生まれた時，父Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leonは40歳，母Jimena Muñozは20歳であった。1094年にポルトガルでConde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonhaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1128年に兵役登録をした。彼女は1130年11月11日にスペインガリシアオウレンゼモンテデラモで50歳で死去し，ポルトガルブラガブラガブラガ スeにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal(이)가 1080년 포르투갈 브라가 po보 데 랜호소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jimena Muñoz(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1094년 포르투갈에서 Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1128년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그녀는 1130년 11월 11일 스페인 갈리샤 우렌스 몽더램오에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 포르투갈 브라가 브라가 브라가 스e에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal nasceu em 1080, em Póvoa de Lanhoso, Braga, Portugal, seu pai, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Jimena Muñoz, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha em 1094, em Portugal. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela alistou-se no serviço militar em 1128. Ela faleceu em 11 de novembro de 1130, em Montederramo, Ourense, Galicia, Espanha, com 50 anos, e foi sepultada em Braga Sé, Braga, Braga, Portugal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal родилась в 1080 г. в местности пoвоа де ланосо, брага, Португалия, её отцу по имени Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Jimena Muñoz, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha в 1094 г. в местности Португалия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. В 1128 году она поступила на военную службу. Она умерла 11 ноября 1130 г. в местности монтедиррамо, оуренс, галися, Испания в возрасте 50 лет и была похоронена в местности брага сe, брага, брага, Португалия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Condesa Teresa de Leon Senhora de Portugal nació en 1080, en Póvoa de Lanhoso, Braga, Portugal, su papá, Alfonso VI de Galicia Rey de Castilla y Leon, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Jimena Muñoz, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Conde de Portugal Henrique de Borgonha en 1094, en Portugal. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 5 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1128. Murió el 11 de noviembre de 1130, en Montederramo, Orense, Galicia, España, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultada en Braga Sé, Braga, Braga, Portugal.
